steps.title: Compute the slope from the table
steps.content: Pick any two rows $(x_1, y_1)$ and $(x_2, y_2)$ from the table and apply $m = \dfrac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$. From the table values, this calculation gives a slope of $m = 6$.

steps.title: Relate the slope to A/B
steps.content: Solving $Ax + By = C$ for $y$ gives $y = -\dfrac{A}{B}x + \dfrac{C}{B}$, so the slope of the line equals $-\dfrac{A}{B}$. Setting $-\dfrac{A}{B} = 6$ yields $\dfrac{A}{B} = \mathbf{-6}$.
